Key Finding: RMS overlaps heavily between normal and subtle anomalies.
Impact on Feature Selection: Do not rely on RMS as the primary descriptor.

Key Finding: Subtle anomalies exhibit persistent tonal components while obvious anomalies contain broadband transients.
Impact on Feature Selection: Need spectral features capable of capturing tonal changes.

Key Finding: Centroid variability (Std/IQR) is more discriminative than centroid mean.
MFCC-1, MFCC-2 and their variability provide the strongest separation. Higher-order MFCCs contribute little.
PCA variance differs from anomaly discrimination; normal behaviour is multimodal.
Impact on Feature Selection: Use supervised feature selection rather than PCA alone.
